mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% in March 2010 reflects post-crisis recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volume indicates accelerating consumer demand and inventory replenishment cycles, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within a 60-day forward window.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355546M in February 2010 shows post-recession recovery momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and forward returns falls below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with rising inflation expectations creates negative real rates that compress equity risk premiums and drive capital reallocation from bonds to equities, producing positive 5-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% and CPIAUCSL at 217.587 in March 2010
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in < 52% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.9862 in February 2010 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 54% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline signals weakening construction demand and reduced future consumer wealth effects, which constrains equity valuations and produces negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 591.0K in February 2010 remains depressed post-housing crisis
regime_hint: housing market contraction with negative wealth effects
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 48% of triggered instances, or correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market recovery that increases household income and consumption, driving positive equity returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129526K and UNRATE at 9.7% in February 2010
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in < 56% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in 2-year rolling logistic regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rate environment reduces borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, lowering discount rates applied to future cash flows and supporting equity valuations with a 60-day forward horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% reflects extended zero-bound policy post-crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth combined with rising retail sales indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, with negative returns predicted when both indicators decelerate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate < 0.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13161.1B and RSAFS at 355546M reflect early recovery momentum
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.
